rationale: The cited paper is not a passing background citation; it is the direct interlocutor for this rejoinder. The citing article explicitly says Ariely, Huber, and Wertenbroch (2005) proposed mechanisms linking intentions and loss aversion, then organizes major discussion sections around those mechanisms (emotional attachment and cognitive focus). The authors use that commentary to frame their conceptual development and add another mechanism (expectations-based reference points), which shows meaningful intellectual carry-forward.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Ariely, Huber, and Wertenbroch (2005) by taking its proposed mediators of intentions and loss aversion as a starting point and elaborating them in a follow-up conceptual argument. It extends that discussion by introducing an additional expectations-based reference-point mechanism.
